content: When using cap_drop: ALL on nginx containers, always add cap_add for SETGID, SETUID, and CHOWN — nginx needs these to fork workers and manage cache directories
Always update OpenAPI contract YAMLs in the same commit as API endpoint changes — downstream consumers symlink to contracts/ and will hit stale specs otherwise
When a feature bead adds or changes API endpoints, include consumer guide and contract YAML updates in the bead scope — never defer docs to a separate follow-up
When a bead scope grows to cover both prose docs and contract YAMLs, split into two beads — one-bead-one-concern prevents sloppy combined deliverables
For critical decisions like architecture reviews or scope expansions, assign the same brief to multiple independent reviewers with no cross-talk — synthesis happens after
When hardening Docker containers with cap_drop ALL, enumerate every Linux capability the entrypoint process uses by checking capability documentation first
Process retro beads briskly — they are pipeline maintenance, not feature work. Extract the learning and close within one session
